req.title: Diagnostic report
req.statement: A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations; missing Observation links shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a clinician can successfully create a DiagnosticReport with valid Observation references (positive/EP), that the system rejects reports with an empty result array with HTTP 422 (negative/NEG), and that the boundary condition of exactly one Observation reference is accepted (boundary/BVA). A fourth test covers the state transition from draft to final status with references present (STC), ensuring the report lifecycle is validated. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and uses deterministic payloads.

tests.name: test_create_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observations
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
At least two Observation resources exist; their IDs available via <observation_ids> fixture
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload with resourceType, status=final, code, subject, and result referencing two Observation IDs
POST payload to /DiagnosticReport with Authorization header
expected: Response body result array length equals 2
trace: R-HLT-012: clinician issues report -> report stored
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observations(base_url, clinician_auth_token, observation_ids):
    obs_refs = [{"reference": f"Observation/{oid}"} for oid in observation_ids[:2]]
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "result": obs_refs
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/DiagnosticReport", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain resource id"
    assert len(body.get("result", [])) == 2, "Result array must contain 2 references"

tests.name: test_create_diagnostic_report_with_empty_result_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload with result set to an empty array
POST payload to /DiagnosticReport with Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error message indicating missing Observation links
trace: R-HLT-012: missing Observation links shall be rejected
R-HLT-012: error pathway -> 422 when references empty
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_diagnostic_report_with_empty_result_rejected(base_url, clinician_auth_token):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "result": []
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/DiagnosticReport", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    has_error = (
        body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
        or "error" in str(body).lower()
        or "result" in str(body).lower()
    )
    assert has_error, "Response must indicate missing Observation references"

tests.name: test_create_diagnostic_report_with_exactly_one_observation
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
At least one Observation resource exists; its ID available via <observation_ids> fixture
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload with result containing exactly one Observation reference (minimum valid cardinality)
POST payload to /DiagnosticReport with Authorization header
expected: Response body result array length equals 1
trace: R-HLT-012: boundary between rejected (0 refs) and accepted (>=1 ref)
R-HLT-012: report stored when exactly one Observation linked
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_diagnostic_report_with_exactly_one_observation(base_url, clinician_auth_token, observation_ids):
    obs_refs = [{"reference": f"Observation/{observation_ids[0]}"}]
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "result": obs_refs
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/DiagnosticReport", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 for single ref, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert len(body.get("result", [])) == 1, "Result must contain exactly 1 reference"

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_state_transition_preliminary_to_final
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
At least one Observation resource exists; its ID available via <observation_ids> fixture
actions: POST a DiagnosticReport with status=preliminary and one Observation reference
Capture the resource id from the 201 response
PUT an updated DiagnosticReport with status=final to /DiagnosticReport/{id}
expected: Initial POST returns HTTP 201 with status=preliminary
trace: R-HLT-012: report stored (both preliminary and final are valid stored states)
R-HLT-012: state transition preliminary->final preserves Observation links
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_diagnostic_report_state_transition_preliminary_to_final(base_url, clinician_auth_token, observation_ids):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    obs_refs = [{"reference": f"Observation/{observation_ids[0]}"}]
    create_payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "preliminary",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "result": obs_refs
    }
    create_resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/DiagnosticReport", json=create_payload, headers=headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201, f"Create failed: {create_resp.text}"
    resource_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    update_payload = {**create_payload, "id": resource_id, "status": "final"}
    update_resp = requests.put(f"{base_url}/DiagnosticReport/{resource_id}", json=update_payload, headers=headers)
    assert update_resp.status_code == 200, f"Update failed: {update_resp.text}"
    assert update_resp.json().get("status") == "final", "Status must be final after transition"
